title: network mode container fails if container use host mode
body:
### Issue Description

Starting a container with network="container:<hash>" will fails if target container use host mode.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman run -d --network=host alpine tail -f /dev/null`
2. `podman run --network="container:<previous-sha>" alpine`


### Describe the results you received

```
Error: crun: cannot setns `/proc/945085/ns/net`: Operation not permitted: OCI permission denied
```


### Describe the results you expected

I expected my new container to be created using the same network: host

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

I tried the same using docker in a clean environment, it works correctly in root and rootless.

Found for https://github.com/nektos/act/issues/303

comments.body: I think I fixed that for pods before, the problem is that we are not allowed to the a setns to the host namspace. However we are still in the host ns so the correct thing for podman is to translate container:xxx to host if the target container runs with --net=host, in this case the setns will be skipped.
